answer:
**Prayascitta** (प्रायश्चित्त), or expiation, refers to ritual penances, vows, baths, gifts, recitations, and other acts prescribed to atone for sins (ranging from major offenses like killing a Brahmin to minor faults like eating impure food), ritual lapses, or impurity (āśauca). It restores purity, caste status, and ancestral satisfaction, often graded by sin severity, caste, intent (intentional vs. unintentional), age, and ability. Performed via self-discipline (e.g., kr̥cchra vows like Cāndrāyaṇa—lunar fasting—or Taptakr̥cchra—hot substances), it may involve assemblies (pariṣad) of learned Brahmins for judgment on unspecified sins.[^1][^2][^3][^4]

Across the texts, prayascitta emphasizes Vedic recitation (e.g., Vyāhṛtis, Gāyatrī), cow products (pañcagavya), baths (especially Gaṅgā), and charity. It varies: Manu systematizes by sin type/caste; Yājñavalkya integrates with āśauca/duties; Yama focuses on specifics like killing/intercourse; Aṅgirasa details assemblies, food/cow penances, and śrāddha faults.

### In Manusmṛti (Manu)
Ch. 11–12 classify sins (mahāpātakas: Brahmin-killing, liquor, gold theft, guru-bed defilement; upapātakas: cow-killing; minor: insect-killing). Penances: 12-year forest exile/skull-banner for Brahmin-killing; boiling in liquor/fire for drinking; Chandrāyaṇa (lunar fast) for theft/guru-bed; cow-following for cow-killing; graded by caste (1/4 for Kṣatriya, 1/8 Vaiśya, 1/16 Śūdra). Unspecified sins via parishad considering ability/sin gravity. Recitation (e.g., Aghamarṣaṇa hymn), gifts, kr̥cchras (Prājāpatya: 3-day phased eating; Sāntapana: cow products; Atikr̥cchra: extended fast). Truth/remorse/tapas purify; Śūdras by service/time.[^5][^6]

### In Yājñavalkya Smṛti
Ch. 3 (prāyaścittādhyāya) frames as purification from āśauca (death/birth impurity: 10–30 days by caste/age; immediate for kings/suicides). Lists purifiers: time/fire/action/earth/wind/mind/knowledge/tapas/water/remorse/fasting. Distress duties allow flexibility (e.g., Brāhmaṇa trades). Penances: bathing/recitation for minor; Chandrāyaṇa/kr̥cchra for grave (e.g., cow-killing). Assembly prescribes per ability/sin; truth/non-violence/senses control as tapas. Broad: yoga/knowledge for ultimate purity; no fixed lists, emphasizes scripture/assembly.[^7]

### In Yama Smṛti
Ch. 1–2 prescribe for sins like suicide attempts (2 Aindava/Cāndrāyaṇa), liquor/beef (Taptakṛcchra/fire-oblation), intercourse (e.g., guru's wife: fire-entry; cow: fortnight Aghamarṣaṇa/milk vow), cow-killing (double govrata). Graded: half for elderly/children/women; Sāntapana for mixed-caste killing. Assemblies (3–10+ Vedins); fines for non-performance. Śūdras: time/charity/service. Vomiting: repeat cooking/piṇḍadāna.[^8][^9]

### In Āṅgirasa Smṛti
Ch. 1 (earlier text) and later Ch. 1–12 detail prayascitta as core, via assemblies (3–100+ Vedins/Aṅgavids). Sins: food (vajra-kr̥cchra 3–10 nights), cow-striking (double govrata), vomiting (homa/recitation). Kr̥cchras (vajra: barley-water; Taptakṛcchra; Cāndrāyaṇa); recitation (Vyāhṛtis/Gāyatrī = bathing/sacrifices); śrāddha faults (redo with Vyāhṛtis). Graded: half for women/children/elderly; Śūdras time/service. Ch. 2–12: approach/confession/pariṣad rules; food/cow penances; no Śūdra gifts/eating.[^10][^11]

Common: Caste/age grading, kr̥cchras/recitation/baths supreme; parishad for doubt; Śūdra food taboo. Differences: Manu most systematic; Aṅgirasa assembly-focused/śrāddha-heavy; Yama/Yājñavalkya concise/practical.

[^1]: Aṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [para p-d769c5](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-d769c5) (prāyaścitta as default where unspecified).
[^2]: Yama Smṛti 1, [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/01/translation/) (penances for sins).
[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti 3, [Ch. 3 intro](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-9dbce5) (prāyaścittādhyāya).
[^4]: Manusmṛti 11–12, [Ch. 12](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/) (sin classification/penances).
[^5]: Manusmṛti 11.44–209[208M], [Ch. 11–12](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/11/translation/) (sins/penances).
[^6]: Ibid., 11.208[207M], [para p-074f6e](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e) (parishad for unspecified).
[^7]: Yājñavalkya 3.31–34, [Ch. 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-879d87) (purifiers); 3.208–209 equiv. (penance ordination).
[^8]: Yama 1.7–8, [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/01/translation/#p-22114f) (krichras).
[^9]: Yama 2.3–4, [Ch. 2](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-bf4911) (fines/non-performance).
[^10]: Aṅgirasa 1 (earlier), [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-d769c5) (Vyāhṛtis default); later Ch. 1–2, [Ch. 1–2](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-38a3ea) (pariṣad/prāyaścitta).
[^11]: Aṅgirasa 2.9–10, [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-82a5bb) (food penances).
